title: Government Information Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Government Information Services 9 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Environmental Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND BUREAU OF PLANNING AND SUSTAINABILITY HOME ENERGY SCORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on the City of Portland's requirement that sellers of most single-family homes obtain and disclose a home energy score at the time of sale. Mobile, manufactured, floating, and multi-family complexes with stacked units are not included in the requirements. Individual homeowners may qualify for an exemption under certain circumstances, including but not limited to foreclosure, condemned or uninhabitable properties, and undue hardship. The Home Energy Score measures a home's energy efficiency based on an on-site evaluation of its physical characteristics. To provide a consistent comparison for home shoppers, it does not measure the actual energy usage of its current occupants. 1810 SW 5th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 278-5347 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMELAND SECURITY UNITED STATES CUSTOMS BORDER PROTECTION I-94 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Government agency for international travelers visiting the United States where they can apply for or retrieve their I-94 admission number and record (proof of legal visitor status) and retrieve a limited travel history of their US arrivals and departures. 1300 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, MS: 1345 Ipl/cbp Info Center Washington, DC 20229 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 5:30am-3pm, closed federal holidays (877) 227-5111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details N11 311 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF In some cities, 311 is an information line for city government or non-emergency police matters. 311 More Details OFFICE OF OREGON GOVERNOR CONSTITUENT SERVICE OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides public access to ask questions or give opinions regarding problems with state agencies and state government functions. Oregonians can engage with the governor's office to share an opinion, request public records, proclamation issuance, military retirement letters, assistance with state government services, ceremonial bill signing, invite the governor to an event, or request a meeting with the governor. The office works to connect people to the appropriate state agency or office to address their concerns. 775 Court Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-4582 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON BLUE BOOK LOCAL GOVERNMENT DIRECTORY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides contact information for city, county, and state government entities. Also offers basic demographic, historical, and tourism information. This directory is intended to provide a primary point of entry for local government offices. 800 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (503) 373-0701 2 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION CLIMATE CHANGE AND HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works with partner agencies to study, prevent and plan for the health effects of climate change. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 673-0482 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about enforcement of laws against discrimination based on race, color, national origin, disability, age, religion, and sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity) by health care and human services. Also provides information, enforcement, and a complaint process for HIPPA for individuals and professionals. 200 Independence Avenue SW Washington, DC 20201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 368-1019 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY UNITED STATES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY REGION 10 ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects individuals from significant risks to human health and the environment through the following core services: -Provides information on what individuals can do in their homes, workplaces, and communities to prevent pollution -Conducts research on environmental issues -Develops and enforcing environmental regulations -Provides information on and working to address pollution of air and water by heavy metals and other hazardous materials -Allocates grants to state environmental programs, nonprofits, educational institutions, etc., addressing environmental issues 1200 6th Avenue Seattle, WA 98101 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-4:30pm Email (206) 553-1200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details USA.GOV FEDERAL CITIZEN INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on federal agencies, programs, benefits, and services. The agency offers information about disasters and emergencies, housing help, disability services, employment and unemployment, taxes, consumer issues, scams and fraud, filing complaints, laws and legal issues, health, travel, immigration and citizenship, military and veterans, education, and voting and elections. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-5pm (844) 872-4681 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND HOME ENERGY SCORE OFFICE OF OREGON GOVERNOR CONSTITUENT SERVICE OFFICE OREGON BLUE BOOK LOCAL GOVERNMENT DIRECTORY OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION CLIMATE CHANGE AND HEALTH
